entities.name: PIGEONPEA
entities.description: A legume crop used for hybrid technology with high yield advantages

entities.name: CENTRAL AND SOUTHERN INDIA
entities.description: The geographic region where pigeonpea hybrids are showing high impact

entities.name: YIELD
entities.description: A trait showing advantages of >30% in pigeonpea hybrids

entities.name: CYTOPLASMIC-NUCLEAR MALE STERILITY
entities.description: A system used as the basis for hybrid pigeonpea technology

entities.name: NUCLEAR DIVERSITY
entities.description: Genetic diversity of the nucleus required for the sustainability of hybrid programs

entities.name: CYTOPLASMIC DIVERSITY
entities.description: Genetic diversity of the cytoplasm required for the sustainability of hybrid programs

entities.name: CMS-INDUCING CYTOPLASMS
entities.description: Cytoplasms that induce male sterility in plants

entities.name: MALE STERILITY
entities.description: A trait inherited maternally in the progeny of a natural hybrid between Cajanus lineatus and Cajanus cajan

entities.name: CAJANUS LINEATUS
entities.description: A wild relative of pigeonpea that provides a male sterility inducing cytoplasm

entities.name: BACKCROSSING
entities.description: A breeding method used to maintain male sterility up to the BC7F1 generation

entities.name: ICPL 99044
entities.description: An advanced breeding pigeonpea line used to maintain male sterility

entities.name: A6
entities.description: The tag assigned to the male sterility inducing cytoplasm of Cajanus lineatus

entities.name: ADAPTATION
entities.description: A desired trait for the development of new pigeonpea hybrids
